files.content:
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
BUILD = ROOT / "build"
BUILD.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

subprocess.check_call(
    [
        "cc",
        "-c",
        str(ROOT / "native_module.c"),
        "-o",
        str(BUILD / "native_module.o"),
    ]
)

reference_fix_files.content:
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
import sysconfig


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
BUILD = ROOT / "build"
BUILD.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
PYTHON_INCLUDE = sysconfig.get_paths()["include"]

subprocess.check_call(
    [
        "cc",
        f"-I{PYTHON_INCLUDE}",
        "-c",
        str(ROOT / "native_module.c"),
        "-o",
        str(BUILD / "native_module.o"),
    ]
)

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Adding Python's include directory to the compiler flags lets cc resolve Python.h during the native build.

reviewer_record.notes: authored by codex; pre-fix fails+matches regex, post-fix passes

files.content:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
SOURCE = ROOT / "src" / "native.c"


def main():
    compiler = os.environ.get("CC", "gcc")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as build_dir:
        output = Path(build_dir) / "native.o"
        command = [compiler, "-c", str(SOURCE), "-o", str(output)]
        completed = subprocess.run(command)

    if completed.returncode:
        print(
            f"error: command '{compiler}' failed with exit code {completed.returncode}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
    return completed.returncode


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Replace the invalid C initializer so gcc can compile the native source successfully.

reviewer_record.notes: authored by codex; pre-fix fails+matches regex, post-fix passes

from setuptools import Extension, setup

setup(
    name="native-broken",
    version="0.1.0",
    packages=["native_broken"],
    ext_modules=[
        Extension("native_broken._speedups", ["native_broken/speedups.c"]),
    ],
)

def add(a, b):
    return a + b

reference_fix_files.content:
from setuptools import setup

setup(
    name="native-broken",
    version="0.1.0",
    packages=["native_broken"],
)

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: The build fails because setup.py declares a native extension that includes a nonexistent system header, so removing that optional extension lets pip build a pure-Python wheel.
